steps.title: Spot the contrast signal
steps.content: The underlined sentence ends with a period, and the next sentence begins with But, a contrast word that signals the following information will challenge or qualify the earlier claim.

steps.title: Match the perception‑challenge function
steps.content:
It describes a common perception of Nixon (being most famous for Watergate) that is then challenged by the later discussion of his complex legacy and other accomplishments.

The other three either treat the underlined sentence as support, an accomplishment, or a comparison, none of which reflect the contrast introduced by But.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
